title: hieracium pilosella subsp. circumvelatum
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. circumvelatum, also known as a variant of the common weed known as mouse-ear hawk's-beard, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Historically, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, Hieracium pilosella subsp. circumvelatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
